content_en:
") Agh-Thariyat 51:56 21 AL-Mu'minun 23:115 '3) AL-Qiyamah 75:36

And there are other Verses which prove that Allah, the Most High has a profound Wisdom in creating the jinns and mankind and that is to worship Him.

Worship is to humble oneself before Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful out of love and glorification, by acting upon His Commands, by avoiding what He has prohibited in the manner dictated by the Islamic Law: Allah, the Most High says:

HE EN Tek Bt iat te

‘And they were commanded not, but that they should worship

Allah, and worship none but Him Alone (abstaining from

ascribing partners to Him),“""! So, this is the wisdom behind the creation of jinns and mankind and accordingly, whoever rebelled against his Lord and arrogantly refused to worship Him, he will have rejected this wisdom, and his action bears witness to (his belief) that Allah created the creation in vain and without purpose. If he claimed that he did not believe that, that still remains a necessary implication of his rebelliousness and his arrogant tefusal to obey his Lord.

Q. 20. How can a person supplicate and not be answered, when Allah says:

“Invoke Me, I will respond to your , invocation).’'?!

A. All praise and thanks are to Allah, the Lord of the worlds, and I send peace and blessings upon our Prophet, Muhammad and upon all his family and Companions. I ask Allah, the Most : High for myself and for my Muslim brothers to grant us success in achieving correctness in belief, in speech and in action; Allah,

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 82 PLY ots I subs the Almighty, the All-Powerful says: Ske & BK OM KB cot wat Heats 305p

kag fe Sis ‘And your Lord said: Invoke Me [i.e. believe in My Oneness (Islamic Monotheism) and ask Me for anything], I will respond to your (invocation). Verily, those who scorn My worship [i.e. do not invoke Me, and do not believe in My Oneness, (Islamic Monotheism)] they will surely enter Hell in humiliation!)

The questioner says that he supplicated to Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful but Allah did not respond to his invocation, and so this fact along with this Noble Verse, in which Allah, the Most High promises those who call upon him that He will respond to their supplication is causing him to doubt but Allah does not break His Promise.

So, the answer to this is that there are conditions attached to the response which must be fulfilled and they are:

1. Sincerity towards Allah, the Most Glorified, the Most High. This means that a person must be sincere in his supplication, tuming towards Allah, the Most Glorified, the Most High with a ready heart and having recourse to Him in truth, knowing that He, the Almighty, the All-Powerful is Able to respond to his invocation, and hoping for a response from Allah, the Most Glorified, the Most High.

2. That when supplicating, a person should feel that he is in the greatest need, indeed the greatest necessity for Allah, the Most Glorified, the Most High and that it is Allah, the Most High, Alone Who responds to the supplication of a person in need when he calls upon Him and removes the evil. But that he should supplicate Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful, while feeling that he is not in need of Allah, the Most Glorified, the Most High

Fatawa on ‘aqeedah (Creed) 83 BLE! gals

and that he has no necessity for Him, and that he is only asking out of habit, then that person is not worthy of being answered.

3. That the one supplicating avoids consuming what is forbidden (usury, stolen money etc.) because devouring what is forbidden acts as an obstacle between a person and the response to his supplication. This is confirmed in the authentic Hadith from the Prophet #% in which it is stated that he said:

2 oe seh pia by Gb yy ie Vv 2b a Sp rental

‘Allah is Good and He therefore accepts only that which is good. And Allah commanded the believers as He commanded the Messengers.”

For He, the Most High says:

“O you who believe! Eat of the lawful things that We have provided you with and be grateful to Allah if it is indeed Him Whom you worship.’"!!

And He, the Most High says: €tas i oct 5 Li “O you Messengers! Eat of the Tayyibat (all kinds of lawful food) and do righteous deeds.’"*!

The Prophet #% then mentioned a person who travels widely, his hair unkempt and covered with dust. He lifts his hands towards the sky (and supplicates thus): ‘Oh, Lord! Oh, Lord!’ whereas * his food is unlawful, his drink is unlawful, and his clothes are unlawful and his nourishment is unlawful. The Prophet 3% said:

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 84 PEM ois I sts

“So how then can his supplication be answered?’

So, the Prophet #% regarded it as unlikely that this man, who fulfilled the outward means by which an invocation is answered would have his supplication answered. Those outward means are:

1. Raising the hands towards the heavens, that is to Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful. Because He, the Most High is in (i.e. above) the heavens, over the Throne; and extending one’s hand to Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful is one of the means of having one’s prayer answered, as is shown by the Hadith narrated by Imam Ahmad in his Musnad:

35 Bl ote fe EN US ES op

“Vane “Verily Allah is Modest and Most Generous and He is too Honorable to return the hands of His slave empty when he raises them to Him.""?!

2. This man called upon Allah, the Most High by the Name of the Lord (Oh, Lord! Oh, Lord!) And entreating Allah, the Most High by this Name is one of the means of having one’s supplication answered. Because the Lord is the Creator, the Owner, the Disposer of all affairs, in His Hand are the keys of the heavens and the earth, and this is why you find that most of the supplications in the Noble Qur’an are in this Name:

"1 Reported by Muslim in the Book of Zakah, in the Chapter on the Virtue of Spending and Charity.

'2] Rreported by Imam Ahmad in his Al-Musnad (5:428) and by AtTirmithi in the Book of Supplications, Chapter 105 (3556) and by Ibn Majah in the Book of Supplication, in the Chapter on Raising the Hands in Supplication (3865).

Fatawa on ‘Agidah (Creed) 85 Baad os ghd

eae) cs SQN ESS OF eadd uae ical ae at

So entreating Allah, the Most High by this Name is one of the means of having it answered.

3. This man was travelling, and travel is in most cases, a means of having a supplication answered. Because when a person is on a journey, he feels that he is in need of Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful and he is more urgent than if he is residing with his family. His hair is unkempt, and he is covered in dust, as if he does not care about himself, as if the most important thing to him is to turn to Allah and supplicate to Him, whatever his situation, whether his hair is unkempt and he is covered in dust, or he is living at ease. Unkempt hair, and being covered in dust have an effect on the response (to supplication). This is confirmed by the Hadith narrated from the Prophet #%, in which it is stated that Allah descends to the lowest heaven in the evening of the Day of ‘Arafah and Allah boasts before the angels about those standing there. He says:

'") Surat Aal ‘Imran 3:193-195

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 86 PEM LIST sobs teat Gb YS be Gel ye EE syle “They have come to Me with hair unkempt and covered in dust, approaching from every deep path.'"") These means for achieving a response to supplication did not result in anything, merely because his food and clothes were unlawful and his sustenance was from the forbidden things. The Prophet 2% said:

“So how then can his supplication be answered?”

Therefore, if these conditions for achieving a response to one’s supplication are not fulfilled, then the supplication is unlikely to be answered. If they are fulfilled and Allah does not answer the one supplicating, it is only due to some wisdom known to Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful, something that is not known to the person supplicating.

It may be that you like a thing which is bad for you.'*! If these conditions are fulfilled but Allah, the Almighty, the AllPowerful does not respond, then it is either to repel from him a greater evil, or it is kept for him until the Day of Resurrection and he is given a reward in full, more and more. This could be because the one supplicating, who asked, fulfilling the conditions, yet he was not answered, and no greater evil was removed from him, but he has fulfilled the means, then the response was prevented due to some wisdom, so he is given a double reward once for his supplication and once for suffering by not having it answered. So, a reward which is greater and more complete is accumulated for him with Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful.

' Reported by Muslim in the Book of Hajj, Chapter on the Virtue of Hajj, ‘Umrah and the Day of ‘Arafah (436). '21 A reference to Surat Al-Baqarah 2:216.

Fatawa on on “aqeedah (Creed) 87 Bad! old

It is also important not to consider the re response slow in coming, for this is also one of the reasons it may not be answered. This is mentioned in the Hadith reported from the Prophet Re:

(jae JG BY Sey

“The supplication of any of you will be answered as long as he is not impatient.”

They said: “How is he impatient, Oh, Messenger of Allah?” He said:

3 Sats oT Lyesy Sye55 S565 bdo “He says: I supplicated, and supplicated, and supplicated, and He did not answer me."""!
content_id:
") Adz-Dzariyat 51:56 21 Al-Mu'minun 23:115 '3) Al-Qiyamah 75:36

Dan ada ayat-ayat lain yang membuktikan bahwa Allah Ta'ala memiliki Hikmah yang mendalam dalam menciptakan jin dan manusia, yaitu untuk beribadah kepada-Nya.

Ibadah adalah merendahkan diri di hadapan Allah Yang Mahakuasa lagi Maha Perkasa dengan penuh cinta dan pengagungan, dengan mengamalkan perintah-perintah-Nya, menjauhi larangan-larangan-Nya sesuai dengan cara yang ditetapkan oleh syariat Islam. Allah Ta'ala berfirman:

HE EN Tek Bt iat te

'Dan mereka tidak diperintahkan kecuali agar mereka beribadah

kepada Allah, dan tidak menyembah selain Dia semata (dengan tidak

menyekutukan-Nya),"""! Maka inilah hikmah di balik penciptaan jin dan manusia. Oleh karena itu, barangsiapa yang memberontak terhadap Tuhannya dan dengan sombong menolak untuk beribadah kepada-Nya, maka ia telah mengingkari hikmah ini, dan perbuatannya menjadi saksi atas (keyakinannya) bahwa Allah menciptakan makhluk dengan sia-sia dan tanpa tujuan. Jika ia mengaku tidak meyakini hal itu, hal tersebut tetap merupakan konsekuensi yang tak terhindarkan dari pemberontakannya dan penolakannya secara sombong untuk menaati Tuhannya.

T. 20. Bagaimana mungkin seseorang berdoa namun tidak dikabulkan, padahal Allah berfirman:

"Berdoalah kepada-Ku, niscaya akan Kuperkenankan bagimu (doamu)."?!

J. Segala puji bagi Allah, Tuhan semesta alam. Shalawat dan salam semoga tercurah kepada Nabi kita Muhammad, beserta seluruh keluarga dan para sahabatnya. Aku memohon kepada Allah Ta'ala bagi diriku dan saudara-saudara Muslimku agar memberikan taufik kepada kami dalam mencapai kebenaran dalam aqeedah, perkataan, dan perbuatan. Allah Yang Mahakuasa lagi Maha Perkasa berfirman:

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 82 PLY ots I subs

kag fe Sis 'Dan Tuhanmu berfirman: Berdoalah kepada-Ku [yaitu berimanlah kepada keesaan-Ku (tawhid) dan mintalah kepada-Ku apa saja], niscaya akan Kuperkenankan bagimu. Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang menyombongkan diri dari beribadah kepada-Ku [yaitu tidak berdoa kepada-Ku dan tidak beriman kepada keesaan-Ku (tawhid)] pasti akan masuk neraka Jahannam dalam keadaan hina!

Penanya mengatakan bahwa ia telah berdoa kepada Allah Yang Mahakuasa lagi Maha Perkasa, namun Allah tidak mengabulkan doanya. Fakta ini beserta ayat mulia yang di dalamnya Allah Ta'ala menjanjikan orang-orang yang berdoa kepada-Nya bahwa Dia akan mengabulkan doa mereka, menyebabkan ia ragu. Padahal Allah tidak pernah mengingkari janji-Nya.

Maka jawaban untuk hal ini adalah bahwa ada syarat-syarat terkait pengabulan doa yang harus dipenuhi, yaitu:

1. Ikhlas kepada Allah Yang Maha Suci lagi Maha Tinggi. Artinya seseorang harus ikhlas dalam doanya, menghadap kepada Allah Yang Maha Suci lagi Maha Tinggi dengan hati yang siap dan bersungguh-sungguh kembali kepada-Nya, mengetahui bahwa Dia Yang Mahakuasa lagi Maha Perkasa mampu mengabulkan doanya, dan berharap akan pengabulan dari Allah Yang Maha Suci lagi Maha Tinggi.

2. Bahwa ketika berdoa, seseorang harus merasa bahwa ia sangat membutuhkan, bahkan sangat bergantung kepada Allah Yang Maha Suci lagi Maha Tinggi, dan bahwa Allah Ta'ala saja yang mengabulkan doa orang yang membutuhkan ketika ia berdoa kepada-Nya dan menghilangkan keburukan. Adapun jika seseorang berdoa kepada Allah Yang Mahakuasa lagi Maha Perkasa sambil merasa tidak membutuhkan Allah Yang Maha Suci lagi Maha Tinggi,

Fatawa on 'aqeedah (Creed) 83 BLE! gals

dan bahwa ia tidak memerlukan-Nya, dan bahwa ia hanya meminta karena kebiasaan semata, maka orang tersebut tidak layak untuk dikabulkan doanya.

3. Bahwa orang yang berdoa menjauhi mengonsumsi yang haram (riba, harta curian, dan sebagainya) karena memakan yang haram menjadi penghalang antara seseorang dengan pengabulan doanya. Hal ini dikuatkan dalam hadith shahih dari Nabi ﷺ yang menyatakan bahwa beliau bersabda:

2 oe seh pia by Gb yy ie Vv 2b a Sp rental

'Sesungguhnya Allah itu Baik dan Dia hanya menerima yang baik. Dan Allah memerintahkan orang-orang beriman sebagaimana Dia memerintahkan para Rasul.'

Karena Dia Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman:

"Wahai orang-orang yang beriman! Makanlah dari rezeki yang baik-baik yang telah Kami berikan kepadamu dan bersyukurlah kepada Allah jika memang hanya kepada-Nya kamu beribadah."!!

Dan Dia Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman: €tas i oct 5 Li "Wahai para Rasul! Makanlah dari makanan yang baik-baik (segala jenis makanan yang halal) dan kerjakanlah amal shalih."*!

Kemudian Nabi ﷺ menyebutkan seseorang yang melakukan perjalanan jauh, rambutnya kusut dan berdebu. Ia mengangkat tangannya ke langit (dan berdoa): 'Ya Tuhan! Ya Tuhan!' Padahal makanannya haram, minumannya haram, pakaiannya haram, dan asupannya haram. Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 84 PEM ois I sts

"Maka bagaimana mungkin doanya akan dikabulkan?"

Maka Nabi ﷺ menganggap mustahil orang ini—yang telah memenuhi sebab-sebab lahiriah agar doa dikabulkan—akan dikabulkan doanya. Sebab-sebab lahiriah tersebut adalah:

1. Mengangkat tangan ke langit, yaitu kepada Allah Yang Mahakuasa lagi Maha Perkasa. Karena Dia Yang Maha Tinggi berada di atas langit, di atas 'Arsy; dan mengulurkan tangan kepada Allah Yang Mahakuasa lagi Maha Perkasa adalah salah satu sebab dikabulkannya doa, sebagaimana ditunjukkan oleh hadith yang diriwayatkan oleh Imam Ahmad dalam Musnad-nya:

35 Bl ote fe EN US ES op

"Vane "Sesungguhnya Allah itu Maha Pemalu dan Maha Pemurah, dan Dia terlalu Mulia untuk mengembalikan tangan hamba-Nya dalam keadaan kosong ketika ia mengangkatnya kepada-Nya.""?!

2. Orang ini berdoa kepada Allah Ta'ala dengan Nama Rabb (Ya Tuhan! Ya Tuhan!) Dan bertawassul kepada Allah Ta'ala dengan Nama ini adalah salah satu sebab dikabulkannya doa. Karena Rabb adalah Sang Pencipta, Sang Pemilik, Sang Pengatur segala urusan, di Tangan-Nya kunci-kunci langit dan bumi. Oleh karena itu, kamu akan mendapati bahwa kebanyakan doa dalam Al-Quran Al-Karim menggunakan Nama ini:

"1 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Zakah, Bab Keutamaan Berinfak dan Bersedekah.

'2] Diriwayatkan oleh Imam Ahmad dalam Al-Musnad-nya (5:428) dan oleh At-Tirmidzi dalam Kitab Doa, Bab 105 (3556) dan oleh Ibnu Majah dalam Kitab Doa, Bab Mengangkat Tangan dalam Berdoa (3865).

Fatawa on 'Aqidah (Creed) 85 Baad os ghd

eae) cs SQN ESS OF eadd uae ical ae at

Maka bertawassul kepada Allah Ta'ala dengan Nama ini adalah salah satu sebab dikabulkannya doa.

3. Orang ini sedang dalam perjalanan, dan safar dalam kebanyakan keadaan merupakan sebab dikabulkannya doa. Karena ketika seseorang dalam perjalanan, ia merasa sangat membutuhkan Allah Yang Mahakuasa lagi Maha Perkasa dan ia lebih mendesak dibanding ketika ia tinggal bersama keluarganya. Rambutnya kusut dan ia berdebu, seolah ia tidak mempedulikan dirinya sendiri, seolah hal terpenting baginya adalah menghadap kepada Allah dan berdoa kepada-Nya, apa pun keadaannya, baik rambutnya kusut dan ia berdebu, maupun ia dalam keadaan nyaman. Rambut yang kusut dan berdebu memiliki pengaruh terhadap pengabulan doa. Hal ini dikuatkan oleh hadith yang diriwayatkan dari Nabi ﷺ, yang menyatakan bahwa Allah turun ke langit dunia pada sore hari 'Arafah dan Allah membanggakan mereka yang berdiri di hadapan para malaikat. Dia berfirman:

'") Surat Ali 'Imran 3:193-195

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 86 PEM LIST sobs teat Gb YS be Gel ye EE syle "Mereka datang kepada-Ku dengan rambut kusut dan berdebu, datang dari setiap jalan yang jauh.'"") Sebab-sebab pengabulan doa ini tidak membuahkan hasil, semata-mata karena makanan dan pakaiannya haram serta rezekinya dari yang diharamkan. Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Maka bagaimana mungkin doanya akan dikabulkan?"

Oleh karena itu, jika syarat-syarat pengabulan doa ini tidak terpenuhi, maka doa tersebut kemungkinan besar tidak akan dikabulkan. Jika syarat-syarat tersebut terpenuhi namun Allah tidak mengabulkan doa orang yang berdoa, maka itu hanyalah karena suatu hikmah yang diketahui oleh Allah Yang Mahakuasa lagi Maha Perkasa, sesuatu yang tidak diketahui oleh orang yang berdoa.

Boleh jadi kamu menyukai sesuatu padahal itu buruk bagimu.'*! Jika syarat-syarat ini terpenuhi namun Allah Yang Mahakuasa lagi Maha Perkasa tidak mengabulkan, maka ada kemungkinan: untuk menolak keburukan yang lebih besar darinya, atau disimpan untuknya hingga Hari Kiamat dan ia diberikan pahala dengan sempurna, bahkan berlipat ganda. Hal ini bisa jadi karena orang yang berdoa telah memenuhi syarat-syarat namun tidak dikabulkan, dan tidak ada keburukan yang lebih besar yang dihindarkan darinya, tetapi ia telah memenuhi sebab-sebabnya. Maka pengabulan ditangguhkan karena suatu hikmah, sehingga ia mendapat dua pahala: sekali karena doanya dan sekali karena menanggung tidak dikabulkannya doa tersebut. Maka pahala yang lebih besar dan lebih sempurna dikumpulkan untuknya di sisi Allah Yang Mahakuasa lagi Maha Perkasa.

Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Hajj, Bab Keutamaan Hajj, 'Umrah dan Hari 'Arafah (436). Merujuk pada Surat Al-Baqarah 2:216.

Fatwa tentang aqeedah (Akidah) 87 Bad! old

Penting juga untuk tidak menganggap jawaban du'a itu lambat datangnya, karena ini juga merupakan salah satu sebab du'a tidak dikabulkan. Hal ini disebutkan dalam hadith yang diriwayatkan dari Nabi ﷺ:

(jae JG BY Sey

"Du'a salah seorang di antara kalian akan dikabulkan selama ia tidak tergesa-gesa."

Mereka bertanya: "Bagaimana ia tergesa-gesa, wahai Rasulullah?" Beliau bersabda:

3 Sats oT Lyesy Sye55 S565 bdo "Ia berkata: Aku telah berdu'a, dan berdu'a, dan berdu'a, namun Dia tidak mengabulkanku."
content_ar: ") Agh-Thariyat 51:56 21 AL-Mu'minun 23:115 '3) AL-Qiyamah 75:36 And there are other Verses which prove that الله, the Most High has a profound Wisdom in creating the jinns and mankind and that is to worship Him. Worship is to humble oneself before الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful out of love and glorification, by acting upon His Commands, by avoiding what He has prohibited in the manner dictated by the الإسلامي Law: الله, the Most High says: HE EN Tek Bt iat te ‘And they were commanded not, but that they should worship الله, and worship none but Him Alone (abstaining from ascribing partners to Him),“""! So, this is the wisdom behind the creation of jinns and mankind and accordingly, whoever rebelled against his الرب and arrogantly refused to worship Him, he will have rejected this wisdom, and his action bears witness to (his belief) that الله created the creation in vain and without purpose. If he claimed that he did not believe that, that still remains a necessary implication of his rebelliousness and his arrogant tefusal to obey his الرب. Q. 20. How can a person supplicate and not be answered, when الله says: “Invoke Me, I will respond to your , invocation).’'?! A. All praise and thanks are to الله, the الرب of the worlds, and I send peace and blessings upon our Prophet, Muhammad and upon all his family and الصحابة. I ask الله, the Most : High for myself and for my المسلم brothers to grant us success in achieving correctness in belief, in speech and in action; الله, الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 82 PLY ots I subs the Almighty, the All-Powerful says: Ske & BK OM KB cot wat Heats 305p kag fe Sis ‘And your الرب said: Invoke Me [i.e. believe in My Oneness (الإسلامي التوحيد) and ask Me for anything], I will respond to your (invocation). Verily, those who scorn My worship [i.e. do not invoke Me, and do not believe in My Oneness, (الإسلامي التوحيد)] they will surely enter النار in humiliation!) The questioner says that he supplicated to الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful but الله did not respond to his invocation, and so this fact along with this Noble Verse, in which الله, the Most High promises those who call upon him that He will respond to their الدعاء is causing him to doubt but الله does not break His Promise. So, the answer to this is that there are conditions attached to the response which must be fulfilled and they are: 1. Sincerity towards الله, the Most Glorified, the Most High. This means that a person must be sincere in his الدعاء, tuming towards الله, the Most Glorified, the Most High with a ready heart and having recourse to Him in truth, knowing that He, the Almighty, the All-Powerful is Able to respond to his invocation, and hoping for a response from الله, the Most Glorified, the Most High. 2. That when supplicating, a person should feel that he is in the greatest need, indeed the greatest necessity for الله, the Most Glorified, the Most High and that it is الله, the Most High, Alone Who responds to the الدعاء of a person in need when he calls upon Him and removes the evil. But that he should supplicate الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful, while feeling that he is not in need of الله, the Most Glorified, the Most High الفتاوى on ‘aqeedah (Creed) 83 BLE! gals and that he has no necessity for Him, and that he is only asking out of habit, then that person is not worthy of being answered. 3. That the one supplicating avoids consuming what is محرم (usury, stolen money etc.) because devouring what is محرم acts as an obstacle between a person and the response to his الدعاء. This is confirmed in the صحيح الحديث from النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% in which it is stated that he said: 2 oe seh pia by Gb yy ie Vv 2b a Sp rental ‘الله is Good and He therefore accepts only that which is good. And الله commanded the believers as He commanded the Messengers.” For He, the Most High says: “O you who believe! Eat of the lawful things that We have provided you with and be grateful to الله if it is indeed Him Whom you worship.’"!! And He, the Most High says: €tas i oct 5 Li “O you Messengers! Eat of the Tayyibat (all kinds of lawful food) and do righteous deeds.’"*! النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% then mentioned a person who travels widely, his hair unkempt and covered with dust. He lifts his hands towards the sky (and supplicates thus): ‘Oh, الرب! Oh, الرب!’ whereas * his food is unlawful, his drink is unlawful, and his clothes are unlawful and his nourishment is unlawful. النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم 3% said: الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 84 PEM ois I sts “So how then can his الدعاء be answered?’ So, النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% regarded it as unlikely that this man, who fulfilled the outward means by which an invocation is answered would have his الدعاء answered. Those outward means are: 1. Raising the hands towards the heavens, that is to الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful. Because He, the Most High is in (i.e. above) the heavens, over the Throne; and extending one’s hand to الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful is one of the means of having one’s الصلاة answered, as is shown by الحديث النبوي الشريف روى by الإمام Ahmad in his Musnad: 35 Bl ote fe EN US ES op “Vane “Verily الله is Modest and Most Generous and He is too Honorable to return the hands of His slave empty when he raises them to Him.""?! 2. This man called upon الله, the Most High by the Name of the الرب (Oh, الرب! Oh, الرب!) And entreating الله, the Most High by this Name is one of the means of having one’s الدعاء answered. Because the الرب is the Creator, the Owner, the Disposer of all affairs, in His Hand are the keys of the heavens and the earth, and this is why you find that most of the supplications in the Noble Qur’an are in this Name: "1 Reported by المسلم in the Book of Zakah, in the Chapter on the الفضيلة of Spending and Charity. '2] Rreported by الإمام Ahmad in his Al-Musnad (5:428) and by AtTirmithi in the Book of Supplications, Chapter 105 (3556) and by ابن ماجه in the Book of الدعاء, in the Chapter on Raising the Hands in الدعاء (3865). الفتاوى on ‘Agidah (Creed) 85 Baad os ghd eae) cs SQN ESS OF eadd uae ical ae at So entreating الله, the Most High by this Name is one of the means of having it answered. 3. This man was travelling, and travel is in most cases, a means of having a الدعاء answered. Because when a person is on a journey, he feels that he is in need of الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful and he is more urgent than if he is residing with his family. His hair is unkempt, and he is covered in dust, as if he does not care about himself, as if the most important thing to him is to turn to الله and supplicate to Him, whatever his situation, whether his hair is unkempt and he is covered in dust, or he is living at ease. Unkempt hair, and being covered in dust have an effect on the response (to الدعاء). This is confirmed by الحديث النبوي الشريف روى from النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #%, in which it is stated that الله descends to the lowest heaven in the evening of the Day of ‘Arafah and الله boasts before the angels about those standing there. He says: '") Surat Aal ‘Imran 3:193-195 الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 86 PEM LIST sobs teat Gb YS be Gel ye EE syle “They have come to Me with hair unkempt and covered in dust, approaching from every deep path.'"") These means for achieving a response to الدعاء did not result in anything, merely because his food and clothes were unlawful and his sustenance was from the محرم things. النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم 2% said: “So how then can his الدعاء be answered?” Therefore, if these conditions for achieving a response to one’s الدعاء are not fulfilled, then the الدعاء is unlikely to be answered. If they are fulfilled and الله does not answer the one supplicating, it is only due to some wisdom known to الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful, something that is not known to the person supplicating. It may be that you like a thing which is bad for you.'*! If these conditions are fulfilled but الله, the Almighty, the AllPowerful does not respond, then it is either to repel from him a greater evil, or it is kept for him until the يوم القيامة and he is given a reward in full, more and more. This could be because the one supplicating, who asked, fulfilling the conditions, yet he was not answered, and no greater evil was removed from him, but he has fulfilled the means, then the response was prevented due to some wisdom, so he is given a double reward once for his الدعاء and once for suffering by not having it answered. So, a reward which is greater and more complete is accumulated for him with الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful.
